weeks.weekly_summary: Establish aerobic base with 4 easy runs and 1 quality session. Focus on rebuilding volume after recent lighter week.
weeks.coaching_notes: Recent Strava data shows ~35 km over 6 runs. Week 1 establishes a sustainable base of 38.5 km across 5 sessions (3.5 hours), leaving headroom within the 7-hour weekly budget. One tempo session (Tuesday) will develop lactate threshold while maintaining 80/20 polarization. Long run on Sunday at 10 km builds foundational endurance. Average pace around 5:59 easy / 5:16 tempo.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 2 increases to ~42 km (+9%) with 2 quality sessions, progressive long run to 11.5 km, and continued easy aerobic emphasis.

days.title: Easy 7km
days.description: Steady aerobic run at easy pace 5:59/km. Focus on relaxed form and steady breathing. This is your foundation work.
days.coach_analysis: Solid execution of your easy run foundation work. You nailed the pace at 6:05/km (only 6 seconds slower than planned) and covered the full 7.64km distance. However, your average HR of 175.7 bpm is significantly elevated for an easy run—aim to keep these aerobic sessions under 160 bpm to ensure you're truly working at a conversational, sustainable intensity.

coach_adjustment.change: Reduce pace to 6:20-6:30/km to bring HR into 155-165 bpm zone
coach_adjustment.reason: Your HR suggests you're running harder than easy intensity; slower pacing will build aerobic base more efficiently and reduce injury risk

days.title: Tempo 2km + 3 × 1km @ 5:16/km
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (5:59/km). Then 3 × 1 km at tempo pace 5:16/km with 90s recovery jog between repeats. Cool-down 1.5 km easy. This develops lactate threshold and teaches you to hold race-relevant effort.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest or gentle cross-training (yoga, stretching). Recovery is where adaptation happens.

days.title: Easy 6.5km
days.description: Recovery-focused easy run at 5:59/km. Short and comfortable. This run bridges toward Sunday's long run.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest day before the long run. Allow central nervous system recovery and glycogen supercompensation.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest or light mobility work. Prepare mentally and physically for Sunday's long run.

days.title: Long Run 10km @ 5:59/km
days.description: Sustained easy effort at long run pace 5:59/km. Focus on controlled breathing and settling into rhythm. This is your weekly aerobic cornerstone. Hydrate and fuel lightly if needed after 45 minutes.
days.notes: Quality session 2 of 2 this week (long run endurance stimulus)

weeks.weekly_summary: Progressive week with 2 quality sessions, volume increase to ~42 km (+9%), and long run growth to 11.5 km. Maintain 80/20 polarization.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 2 increases total distance by ~9% (from 38.5 to 42 km) while staying well within the 7-hour budget (~4.2 hours). Tuesday introduces fartlek drills (unstructured intervals) to build aerobic power; Thursday adds a second tempo block. Long run grows to 11.5 km (+1.5 km from W1), maintaining the sustainable 1-2 km/week growth pattern. Easy runs remain at 5:59/km, quality work at 5:16/km tempo pace.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 3 peaks with volume ~44-45 km (+7% from W2), a longer long run (13 km), and 2 harder sessions. This is the highest training stress before Week 4 recovery.

days.title: Easy 7km
days.description: Steady aerobic run at easy pace 5:59/km. Relax and enjoy the movement.

days.title: Fartlek: 6 × (3min hard @ 5:20/km + 2min easy)
days.description: Warm-up 1.5 km easy (5:59/km). Then 6 × (3 minutes at controlled hard effort ~5:20/km + 2 minutes easy jog recovery). Cool-down 1 km easy. Fartlek teaches pace awareness and builds aerobic power in an unstructured, natural rhythm.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest. Recover from Tuesday's fartlek session.

days.title: Tempo 1.5km + 4 × 1km @ 5:16/km
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (5:59/km). Then 1.5 km sustained at tempo pace 5:16/km, followed by 4 × 1 km at 5:16/km with 90s recovery between repeats. Cool-down 1 km easy. Progressive threshold work building consistency at race-relevant effort.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest before the long run. Allow nervous system recovery.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest and mental preparation for Sunday.

days.title: Long Run 11.5km @ 5:59/km
days.description: Sustained easy run at long run pace 5:59/km. Maintain steady breathing and relaxed form over the extended distance. This builds mitochondrial density and aerobic endurance. Hydrate and fuel as needed.
days.notes: Long run progression +1.5 km from Week 1

weeks.weekly_summary: Peak training week with volume ~45 km (+7% from Week 2), long run 13 km, and 2 quality sessions. This is the highest stress block before taper/recovery.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 3 marks the cumulative peak of this 4-week block. Total volume reaches ~45 km (4.5 hours, within budget). Long run grows to 13 km (+1.5 km), approaching sustainable marathon-specific endurance. Tuesday brings structured intervals (VO2 max work at ~5:15/km pace); Thursday returns to tempo to reinforce lactate threshold. Easy runs hold at 5:59/km. Two quality sessions remain spaced 48+ hours apart. After this peak, Week 4 cuts 20-25% for recovery and adaptation.
weeks.next_week_preview: Week 4 is a recovery week — volume drops to ~34-35 km (−24%), long run shortens to ~10.5 km, but 1 quality session is retained to maintain neuromuscular stimulus.

days.title: Easy 7.5km
days.description: Comfortable aerobic run at easy pace 5:59/km. Settle into rhythm and enjoy the foundation work.

days.title: Intervals: 5 × 1km @ 5:15/km + 2min recovery
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (5:59/km). Then 5 × 1 km at VO2 max effort ~5:15/km with 2-minute recovery jog between repeats. Cool-down 1 km easy. This session builds cardiovascular capacity and aerobic power needed for marathon racing.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest after Tuesday's hard intervals.

days.title: Tempo 2km + 3 × 1.5km @ 5:16/km
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (5:59/km). Then 2 km sustained tempo at 5:16/km, followed by 3 × 1.5 km tempo repeats at 5:16/km with 90s recovery jogs. Cool-down 0.5 km easy. Extended threshold work reinforces lactate clearance and race-specific aerobic power.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Complete rest before Sunday's long run.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest and mental focus for Sunday.

days.title: Long Run 13km @ 5:59/km
days.description: Extended easy aerobic run at long run pace 5:59/km. This distance builds significant endurance and aerobic adaptation. Maintain relaxed form and steady breathing. Fuel and hydrate as needed. This is your longest run of the block.
days.notes: Peak long run: +1.5 km from Week 2

weeks.weekly_summary: Recovery week: volume drops to ~34 km (−24% from Week 3). Retain 1 quality session for neuromuscular maintenance; reduce long run by 20% to 10.5 km. Prepare for next block progression.
weeks.coaching_notes: Week 4 is the mandatory recovery week following the 3-week build. Total volume drops to ~34 km (3.4 hours), staying within budget and allowing full central nervous system and muscular adaptation. The long run reduces to 10.5 km (20% cut from Week 3's 13 km). One quality session (Tuesday tempo) is retained at moderate intensity to prevent detraining. Easy runs remain at 5:59/km. This recovery phase is critical — do not skip it. You'll emerge Week 5 fresher, stronger, and ready for the next training block.
recovery_flags: Recovery week — maintain easy paces, avoid intensity creep
Long run reduced to 10.5 km for regeneration
Only 1 quality session at moderate tempo intensity
weeks.next_week_preview: Next block (Week 5+) will increase volume and introduce marathon-pace work. Recovery in Week 4 ensures you can tolerate increased training stress without injury or chronic fatigue.

days.title: Easy 6.5km
days.description: Relaxed aerobic run at easy pace 5:59/km. This is recovery work — no intensity needed.

days.title: Tempo 2km + 2 × 1km @ 5:20/km
days.description: Warm-up 2 km easy (5:59/km). Then 2 km sustained at moderate tempo ~5:20/km, followed by 2 × 1 km at 5:20/km with 2-minute recovery jogs. Cool-down 1 km easy. This is a maintenance session — lower volume and intensity than Weeks 1–3 to preserve neuromuscular adaptation without excessive fatigue.
days.notes: Only quality session this week; moderate intensity for recovery week

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest and active recovery. Stretch, foam roll, or light mobility work.

days.title: Easy 6km
days.description: Gentle aerobic run at easy pace 5:59/km. Shorter than Week 1–3 to support recovery.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest and prepare for Sunday.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Rest before the recovery long run.

days.title: Long Run Recovery 10.5km @ 5:59/km
days.description: Recovery-paced long run at easy pace 5:59/km. This is 20% shorter than Week 3 to allow full regeneration. Enjoy the distance at a relaxed effort. Hydrate and fuel lightly as needed.
days.notes: Recovery long run: reduced by 20% from Week 3's 13 km

coaching_overview: This 4-week Base Building block establishes a sustainable aerobic foundation (80/20 polarization) with progressive volume and intensity. Week 1 (38.5 km, 3.5 hrs) rebuilds from recent lighter training. Week 2 (42 km) introduces fartlek and tempo work. Week 3 (45 km, peak) includes VO2 max intervals and extended tempo; long run reaches 13 km. Week 4 (34 km, recovery) reduces volume by 24% and retains only 1 moderate-intensity session. All sessions use your current fitness paces: Easy 5:59/km, Tempo 5:16/km, VO2max intervals ~5:15/km. Total training load stays within your 7-hour weekly budget. After this block, the next phase (Aerobic Development → Peak Training) will introduce marathon-pace work and longer intervals. No race date is set, so plan flexibly — this block repeats or evolves based on race timing.
